items.title: HERMES++: Toward a Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation
items.summary: HERMES++ integrates 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction for autonomous driving.
items.why_matters: This work addresses a critical gap in autonomous driving technology by combining scene understanding with future predictions.
items.connection: The focus on driving world models aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in end-to-end driving stacks and world models.
items.proposal_angle: This could enhance the NSF CSR proposal on predictable DNN inference by improving scene understanding.
items.student_project: The integration of scene understanding and prediction could support student projects on VLA models for driving.
items.rationale: Strong alignment with ongoing proposals and significant advancements in driving world models.

items.title: OmniRobotHome: A Multi-Camera Platform for Real-Time Multiadic Human-Robot Interaction
items.summary: OmniRobotHome is a platform for real-time multiadic human-robot interaction using synchronized RGB cameras and coordinated robotic arms.
items.why_matters: It enables effective collaboration in shared spaces, addressing challenges of occlusion and real-time tracking.
items.connection: This work aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in multi-agent systems and real-time perception for autonomous robots.
items.proposal_angle: It could enhance the safety and interaction aspects of the NSF CSR proposal on DNN inference for autonomous vehicles.
items.student_project: The platform's focus on real-time perception and human behavior modeling fits well with student projects on multimodal fusion and perception.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to multi-agent systems and real-time perception in autonomy.

items.title: FlexiTac: A Low-Cost, Open-Source, Scalable Tactile Sensing Solution for Robotic Systems
items.summary: FlexiTac is a low-cost, scalable tactile sensing solution for robotic systems that supports modern tactile learning pipelines.
items.why_matters: It enhances tactile sensing capabilities for robotics, crucial for improving autonomy and manipulation tasks.
items.connection: This aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in tactile sensing and multimodal perception for autonomous systems.
items.proposal_angle: It could support the NSF CIRC proposal by enhancing tactile learning pipelines for cloud-edge-robot co-inference.
items.student_project: It fits well with student projects focused on sensing and multimodal fusion at the edge.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to tactile sensing and autonomy, valuable for ongoing proposals.

items.title: LaST-R1: Reinforcing Action via Adaptive Physical Latent Reasoning for VLA Models
items.summary: LaST-R1 integrates latent reasoning and reinforcement learning for improved VLA model performance in robotic manipulation.
items.why_matters: This work enhances adaptability and generalization in VLA models, crucial for autonomous systems.
items.connection: The paper's focus on VLA models aligns with Prof. Liu's research on foundation models for autonomy and reinforcement learning.
items.proposal_angle: The integration of latent reasoning could enhance the proposed NSF CSR project on predictable DNN inference for real-time autonomous vehicles.
items.student_project: The adaptive latent CoT mechanism could be explored in student projects focused on VLA foundation models for end-to-end driving.
items.rationale: Strong alignment with research interests and potential to enhance ongoing proposals.

items.title: Robust Fusion of Object-Level V2X for Learned 3D Object Detection
items.summary: This paper explores integrating V2X communication into 3D object detection for automated driving, enhancing robustness against real-world conditions.
items.why_matters: Improving object detection in autonomous vehicles can enhance safety and reliability, especially in challenging environments.
items.connection: The focus on V2X communication and perception aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in autonomous driving and multimodal perception.
items.proposal_angle: This work could support the NSF CSR proposal on predictable DNN inference by improving perception under adverse conditions.
items.student_project: The integration of V2X information into perception systems could align with student projects on BEV perception under sensor noise.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to autonomy and perception, with potential for future proposals.

items.title: RopeDreamer: A Kinematic Recurrent State Space Model for Dynamics of Flexible Deformable Linear Objects
items.summary: The paper presents a model for predicting the dynamics of flexible deformable linear objects in robotic manipulation tasks.
items.why_matters: This work addresses challenges in manipulating flexible objects, which is crucial for advanced robotics applications.
items.connection: The focus on manipulation aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in mobile manipulation and dexterous manipulation.
items.student_project: This project could support student work on manipulation planning and robotics.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to manipulation in robotics and fits well with student projects.

items.title: Robotics Institute Launches Next Phase of Vision-Language-Navigation Challenge
items.summary: The latest phase of the Vision-Language-Navigation Challenge aims to enhance robots' ability to understand and act on human instructions.
items.why_matters: This challenge addresses a critical aspect of robotics, improving human-robot interaction and navigation capabilities.

items.student_project: This challenge could provide valuable insights for student projects focused on VLA models and real-world navigation.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to vision-language-action models and student projects.

items.title: RunAgent: Interpreting Natural-Language Plans with Constraint-Guided Execution
items.summary: RunAgent is a multi-agent platform that interprets natural-language plans with constraint-guided execution.
items.why_matters: It enhances the reliability of LLMs for structured workflow execution, crucial for autonomous systems.
items.connection: RunAgent's focus on structured execution aligns with Prof. Liu's interest in VLA models and autonomous systems.
items.student_project: RunAgent's approach could support student projects on VLA models and real-time execution.
items.rationale: The paper's focus on structured execution is relevant to ongoing student research.

items.title: BOLT: Online Lightweight Adaptation for Preparation-Free Heterogeneous Cooperative Perception
items.summary: BOLT is a lightweight module for preparation-free heterogeneous cooperative perception using ego-as-teacher distillation.
items.why_matters: It enables real-time cooperation among independently trained agents without prior coordination, enhancing perception capabilities.
items.connection: This work aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in multimodal perception and collaborative intelligence in autonomous systems.
items.proposal_angle: It could enhance the cloud-edge-robot collaborative inference proposal by improving perception in dynamic environments.
items.student_project: This research could support student projects focused on multimodal fusion and real-time perception under adverse conditions.
items.rationale: Strong relevance to cooperative perception in autonomous systems.

items.title: Global Optimality for Constrained Exploration via Penalty Regularization
items.summary: The paper proposes a Policy Gradient Penalty method for constrained exploration in reinforcement learning.
items.why_matters: This method addresses real-world exploration challenges in reinforcement learning, particularly under constraints.
items.connection: The work aligns with Prof. Liu's interests in reinforcement learning and autonomous systems.
items.proposal_angle: It could enhance the exploration strategies in the NSF CSR proposal on DNN inference for autonomous vehicles.
items.student_project: This could serve as a basis for student projects on reinforcement learning and exploration strategies.
items.rationale: Relevant to reinforcement learning in autonomy, with potential applications in ongoing proposals.

items.title: Video Friday: Figure, 1X Ramp Up Humanoid Robot Production
items.summary: Figure has begun full-scale production of humanoid robots for various applications.

items.title: Map2World: Segment Map Conditioned Text to 3D World Generation
items.summary: Map2World introduces a framework for 3D world generation based on user-defined segment maps, enhancing detail and coherence.

items.title: Strait: Perceiving Priority and Interference in ML Inference Serving
items.summary: Strait enhances deadline satisfaction for dual-priority inference traffic in ML inference serving systems under high GPU utilization.
items.why_matters: Improving latency and task prioritization in ML inference can enhance real-time performance in autonomous systems.

items.title: Red-teaming a network of agents: Understanding what breaks when AI agents interact at scale

items.title: PhyCo: Learning Controllable Physical Priors for Generative Motion
items.summary: PhyCo introduces controllable physical priors into video generation for improved physical consistency.

items.title: AI Processing of Earth Images Can Now Run in Space
items.summary: AI image processing onboard satellites enables real-time object classification from space.

items.title: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows
items.summary: Claw-Eval-Live is a live benchmark for evaluating workflow agents against evolving demands and verifiable actions.

items.title: Generalizable Sparse-View 3D Reconstruction from Unconstrained Images
items.summary: GenWildSplat is a framework for reconstructing 3D scenes from sparse, unposed images without per-scene optimization.
items.why_matters: This work addresses challenges in 3D scene reconstruction, which is relevant for perception in autonomous systems.
items.connection: The paper's focus on 3D reconstruction relates to multimodal perception and sensor fusion in autonomous systems.
items.student_project: This work could inform student projects on perception under adverse conditions.

items.title: A Comparative Analysis of Machine Learning Models for Intrusion Detection in Intelligent Transport Systems
items.summary: The paper proposes a trust-aware federated hybrid intrusion detection framework for Intelligent Transportation Systems using edge computing.

items.title: Phase stability regulator based on two dynamic parameters for autonomous mobile robots
items.summary: A phase regulator using two real-time signals can enhance the performance of autonomous mobile robots.

items.title: Meta buys robotics startup to bolster its humanoid AI ambitions

items.title: Tempus: A Temporally Scalable Resource-Invariant GEMM Streaming Framework for Versal AI Edge
items.summary: Tempus is a scalable GEMM framework for efficient edge AI deployment on AMD Versal SoCs.

items.title: Persistent Visual Memory: Sustaining Perception for Deep Generation in LVLMs
items.summary: The paper introduces Persistent Visual Memory (PVM) to enhance visual perception in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs).
items.why_matters: PVM addresses visual attention decay in LVLMs, improving performance in multimodal tasks.

items.title: Let ViT Speak: Generative Language-Image Pre-training
items.summary: GenLIP is a generative pretraining framework for Vision Transformers that aligns vision encoders with autoregressive language models.

items.title: Visual Generation in the New Era: An Evolution from Atomic Mapping to Agentic World Modeling

items.title: Posterior Augmented Flow Matching
items.summary: Posterior-Augmented Flow Matching improves flow matching by reducing gradient variance during training with an unbiased estimator.
items.why_matters: This method enhances training efficiency for generative models, which could be relevant for autonomy applications.
items.connection: The paper's focus on improving generative models may relate to foundation models for autonomy in Prof. Liu's research.

items.title: Exploration Hacking: Can LLMs Learn to Resist RL Training?
items.summary: The paper investigates 'exploration hacking' in reinforcement learning for large language models, highlighting potential failure modes.

items.connection: The exploration hacking concept relates to reinforcement learning, which is relevant to Prof. Liu's work on VLA models and autonomous systems.
items.rationale: Relevant to RL and autonomy, but not directly applicable to current proposals or student projects.

items.title: Continuous-tone Simple Points: An $\ell_0$-Norm of Cyclic Gradient for Topology-Preserving Data-Driven Image Segmentation
items.summary: The paper proposes a method for topology-preserving data-driven image segmentation using continuous-valued images.

items.title: Design and Characteristics of a Thin-Film ThermoMesh for the Efficient Embedded Sensing of a Spatio-Temporally Sparse Heat Source
items.summary: ThermoMesh is a thin-film thermoelectric sensor for detecting sparse heat sources through thermal imaging.
items.why_matters: It enables efficient thermal sensing in challenging environments, which could enhance robotic perception.
items.connection: The work on thermal sensing could relate to multimodal perception in autonomous systems.

items.title: Action Motifs: Self-Supervised Hierarchical Representation of Human Body Movements
items.summary: The paper presents A4Mer, a self-supervised hierarchical representation for modeling human body movements using a novel dataset.

items.title: Automating GPU Kernel Translation with AI Agents: cuTile Python to cuTile.jl

items.title: Scale-Aware Adversarial Analysis: A Diagnostic for Generative AI in Multiscale Complex Systems
items.summary: The paper introduces a diagnostic framework for evaluating generative AI in multiscale complex systems using physically constrained data generation.

items.title: An adaptive wavelet-based PINN for problems with localized high-magnitude source
items.summary: The paper presents an adaptive wavelet-based PINN to solve differential equations with localized high-magnitude source terms effectively.
items.why_matters: This method addresses loss imbalance in physics-informed neural networks, which is relevant for various physical applications.
items.connection: The adaptive wavelet-based PINN could enhance physics-aware learning in autonomy systems, though its direct application is unclear.

items.title: Crab: A Semantics-Aware Checkpoint/Restore Runtime for Agent Sandboxes
items.summary: Crab is a runtime that improves checkpointing for autonomous agents by bridging the agent-OS semantic gap.

items.title: PhysiGen: Integrating Collision-Aware Physical Constraints for High-Fidelity Human-Human Interaction Generation
items.summary: PhysiGen proposes a method to integrate collision-aware physical constraints for realistic human-human interaction generation in 3D motion synthesis.

items.title: Why physical AI is the real manufacturing revolution
items.summary: Physical AI aims to revolutionize manufacturing by addressing scaling challenges in robotics.

items.title: Can Coding Agents Reproduce Findings in Computational Materials Science?

items.title: Synthetic Computers at Scale for Long-Horizon Productivity Simulation

summary.headline: Advancements in VLA Models and Tactile Sensing for Robotics
summary.tldr: Today's research emphasizes the integration of vision-language-action models with tactile sensing solutions for enhanced robotic interaction and manipulation. Notable works include HERMES++ for driving world models and FlexiTac for scalable tactile sensing.
